title: viola stagnina var. schultzii
intro: Viola stagnina var. schultzii, also known as Marsh Violet or Marsh Fawn's-foot, is a perennial plant that belongs to the violet family. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and its leaves, flowers, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant is known for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Viola stagnina var. schultzii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat colds, coughs, and skin conditions, as well as to promote healthy digestion and reduce stress.
